---
title: "0_Apex_Data_Extraction"
author: "HM Putnam"
date: "2026-05-29"
output: html_document
---
For APEX data, the script is based on Zoe Dellart's Protocol<https://github.com/zdellaert/TimeSeries/blob/main/protocols/Apex_Download.md> and script <https://github.com/zdellaert/TimeSeries/blob/main/3-Pacu/code/DMs.Rmd>.

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
library(tidyverse)
library("XML")
library("plyr")
library(lubridate) # used for converting 8 digit date into datetime format for R
library(oce)
library(ggpmisc)

#library(RColorBrewer)
```


Read in xml info
```{r}
apex.xmlfile <- xmlParse("http://10.10.204.74/cgi-bin/datalog.xml?sdate=260422&days=2") #read in the date (e.g. 180620) plus # days (e.g. days=4) of Apex data
Apex.Data <- ldply(xmlToList(apex.xmlfile), data.frame) #convert xml to dataframe
```

# clean,  arrange, and save data
```{r}
# Select columns of interest by name from df
#We need each probe name from the Apex listed here
#We are using the following nomenclature Temperature = Tmp_#, Conductivity = Cond_#, pH = pH_#
probes <- c("Tmp_1", "Tmp_2", "Tmp_3", "Tmp_4", "Tmp_5","Cond_1", "Cond_2", "Cond_3", "Cond_4", "Cond_5","pH_1", "pH_2", "pH_3", "pH_4", "pH_5")

#look for the column that has the probe name (e.g., Tmp_3)
#Find matching probe.name columns
probe_name_cols <- grep("^probe\\.name", names(Apex.Data), value = TRUE)

#map the probe names to columns
probe_map <- sapply(probe_name_cols, function(col) {
  na.omit(Apex.Data[[col]])[1]
})

probe_map

#select desired probes
matched_name_cols <- names(probe_map)[probe_map %in% probes]

matched_value_cols <- sub("probe\\.name", "probe.value", matched_name_cols)

Apex.sub <- Apex.Data[, c("date", matched_value_cols)]

#rename probes from map
new_names <- probe_map[matched_name_cols]

Apex.sub <- Apex.Data[, c("date", matched_value_cols)]
names(Apex.sub) <- c("date", new_names)

str(Apex.sub)

# Convert date string to POSIXct with correct time zone
Apex.sub <- Apex.sub %>%
  mutate(date = as.POSIXct(date, format = "%m/%d/%Y %H:%M:%S", tz = "America/Los_Angeles"))

Apex.sub <- Apex.sub %>%
  mutate(time = date + hours(3))

#Apex.sub$time <- Apex.sub$date + 3 * 60 * 60
#str(Apex.sub)

# Remove NAs
Apex.sub <- na.omit(Apex.sub)

# Format file name with today's date
today_date <- format(Sys.Date(), "%Y%m%d")

# Write cleaned data to CSV
write.csv(Apex.sub, paste0("Data/", today_date, "_", "ApexRawData.csv"), row.names = FALSE)

```
#convert to salinity 
```{r}
# conductivity in mS/cm
# temperature in Â°C

#change all chr to numeric
Apex.sub <- Apex.sub %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    dplyr::across(
      .cols = c(starts_with("Tmp_"), starts_with("pH_"), starts_with("Cond_")),
      .fns = as.numeric
    )
  )

Apex.sub$pressure <- 0

str(Apex.sub)

swSCTp(
  conductivity = 50,
  temperature = 25,
  pressure = 0,
  conductivityUnit = "mS/cm")


Apex.sub <- Apex.sub %>%
  mutate(Sal_1 = swSCTp(
  conductivity = Cond_1,
  temperature = Tmp_1,
  pressure = pressure,
  conductivityUnit = "mS/cm"),
  Sal_2 = swSCTp(
  conductivity = Cond_2,
  temperature = Tmp_2,
  pressure = pressure,
  conductivityUnit = "mS/cm"),
  Sal_3 = swSCTp(
  conductivity = Cond_3,
  temperature = Tmp_3,
  pressure = pressure,
  conductivityUnit = "mS/cm"),
  Sal_4 = swSCTp(
  conductivity = Cond_4,
  temperature = Tmp_4,
  pressure = pressure,
  conductivityUnit = "mS/cm"))
  

```

## APEX plots
```{r}

#check for outliers
Apex.sub <- Apex.sub %>%
  mutate(across(-time, as.numeric))
str(Apex.sub)

outlier_rows <- Apex.sub %>%
  filter(
    if_any(matches("^Tmp_"),  ~ . < 15 | . > 35) |
    if_any(matches("^pH_"),   ~ . < 7.5 | . > 9.0) |
    if_any(matches("^Cond_"), ~ . < 20 | . > 70)|
    if_any(matches("^Sal_"), ~ . < 30 | . > 50)
  )

outlier_rows

#remove outliers
Apex.sub.clean <- Apex.sub %>%
  filter(
    !(
      if_any(matches("^Tmp_"),  ~ . < 15 | . > 35) |
      if_any(matches("^pH_"),   ~ . < 6.5 | . > 9.0) |
      if_any(matches("^Cond_"), ~ . < 20 | . > 70)|
    if_any(matches("^Sal_"), ~ . < 30 | . > 50)
    )
  )

#truncate data from start time to stop time per trial
start_time <- as.POSIXct("2026-04-22 13:30:00", tz = "America/New_York")
end_time   <- as.POSIXct("2026-04-23 12:40:00", tz = "America/New_York")

Apex.sub.trim <- Apex.sub.clean %>%
  filter(time >= start_time &
         time <= end_time)

probe_treatment_map <- tibble(
  probe = c("1", "2", "3", "4"),
  treatment = c("Control", "Control", "High", "High")  # edit these
)

Apex.sub.trim <- Apex.sub.trim %>% 
  subset(select = c(-date, -pressure))

Apex.long <- Apex.sub.trim %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = - time,
    names_to = "probe_name",
    values_to = "value"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    value = as.numeric(value),
    parameter = case_when(
      str_detect(probe_name, "^Tmp")  ~ "Temperature",
      str_detect(probe_name, "^pH")   ~ "pH",
      str_detect(probe_name, "^Cond") ~ "Conductivity",
      str_detect(probe_name, "^Sal") ~ "Salinity"
    ),
    probe = str_extract(probe_name, "\\d+$")
  ) %>%
  left_join(probe_treatment_map, by = "probe")

str(Apex.long)

apex_data <- ggplot(Apex.long, aes(x = time, y = value, color = treatment, shape = probe)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 1, size = 1.5) +
  facet_wrap(~ parameter, scales = "free_y", ncol = 1) +
  scale_x_datetime(
  date_breaks = "2 hour",
  date_labels = "%b %d\n%H:%M"
)+
    scale_color_manual(
    values = c(
      "Control" = "blue",
      "High" = "red"
    ))+
  theme_bw() +
  labs(
    x = "Date",
    y = "Value",
    color = "Treatment",
    shape = "Probe"
  )
apex_data

# Save plot 
ggsave("Output/Trial_1_apex_data.png", apex_data, width = 10, height = 8, units = c("in"))

```
#calculate the rate of change of salinity
```{r}

ggplot(Apex.sub.trim, aes(x = time, y = Sal_3)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 1, size = 1.5) +
   geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE, color = "red") +
  stat_poly_eq(
    formula = y ~ x,
    aes(label = paste(..eq.label.., ..rr.label.., sep = "~~~~")),
    parse = TRUE
  )+
  scale_x_datetime(
  date_breaks = "2 hour",
  date_labels = "%b %d\n%H:%M"
)+
  theme_bw() +
  labs(
    x = "Date",
    y = "Salinity psu"
  )
  
rate.mods <- summary(lm(Sal_3~time, Apex.sub.trim ))

#convert the slope of psu per second into psu/hr
0.000152 * 60 * 60



```


#OXY DATA
```{r}
#reading oxy files
Oxy1 <- read.csv("Data/PreSens_OxyData/20260422_B2_CH1.csv", fileEncoding = "latin1")
Oxy2 <- read.csv("Data/PreSens_OxyData/20260422_B4_CH2.csv", fileEncoding = "latin1")
Oxy3 <- read.csv("Data/PreSens_OxyData/20260422_A2_CH3.csv", fileEncoding = "latin1")
Oxy4 <- read.csv("Data/PreSens_OxyData/20260422_A4_CH5.csv", fileEncoding = "latin1")


#adding columns in the dataframes
Oxy1$Chamber <- "Oxy_1"
Oxy2$Chamber <- "Oxy_2"
Oxy3$Chamber <- "Oxy_3"
Oxy4$Chamber <- "Oxy_4"

# Combine into one dataframe
oxy_everything <- bind_rows(Oxy1, Oxy2, Oxy3, Oxy4)

#select columns needed for the analysis 
oxy_final <- oxy_everything %>%
  select(Date, Chamber, Oxygen, Temperature) #%>%
 #filter(!(Chamber == "Oxy_2" & Oxygen > 100))

# Convert datetime column
oxy_final$Date <- as.POSIXct(
  oxy_final$Date,
  format = "%d/%m/%y %H:%M:%S"
)

str(oxy_final)

oxy_treatment_map <- tibble(
  Chamber = c("Oxy_1", "Oxy_2", "Oxy_3", "Oxy_4"),
  treatment = c("Control", "Control", "High", "High")
)

oxy.long <- oxy_final %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(Oxygen, Temperature),
    names_to = "parameter",
    values_to = "value"
  ) %>%
  left_join(oxy_treatment_map, by = "Chamber")

oxy_plot <- ggplot(
  oxy.long,
  aes(
    x = Date,
    y = value,
    color = treatment,
    shape = Chamber
  )
) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.5, size = 1.5) +
  facet_wrap(
    ~parameter,
    scales = "free_y",
    ncol = 1
  ) +
  scale_color_manual(
    values = c(
      "Control" = "blue",
      "High" = "red"
    )
  ) +
  scale_x_datetime(
    date_breaks = "2 hour",
    date_labels = "%b %d\n%H:%M"
  ) +
  theme_bw() +
  labs(
    x = "Date",
    y = "Value",
    color = "Treatment",
    shape = "Chamber"
  ) +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(
      angle = 45,
      hjust = 1
    )
  )

oxy_plot

# Save plot 
ggsave("Output/Trial1_Oxygen_Temp.png", oxy_plot, width = 10, height = 8, units = c("in"))


```
